title: Comparing niceness of cities in America - Request

transcript.text: Hello, here's a video request.
These are my different video requests, by the way.
Gotta do one on these dudes.
But I want to, like, watch the video again,
Because it's been a...
I watched it once, and it's been a while.
But I'll do this one, because it's easy.
Somebody goes,
In what towns have you met the coolest people?
Um...
Okay.
Um...
Las Vegas, they were really, really, uh, outgoing,
But it seemed like...
I didn't feel comfortable there.
I felt like it was kind of in-your-face, intrusive.
And that's some, like, freaky scam artist dude
Who freaked me out,
Because he, like, was telling me how he made, like,
All this, like, $10,000 a month,
And then he wanted to borrow money from me, $10,
And he kept emailing me and calling me,
And...
Fucking weird, man.
It was, like, obvious that he was just a scam artist.
But, uh, yeah, they're really friendly.
They're really nice in, uh, Phoenix.
Really mellow.
Seattle.
Oh, man.
I forgot to say Seattle.
Seattle.
They're the coolest.
They're really friendly up here in Washington.
All over Washington.
And, uh, Austin, they're really friendly.
In what towns have you met the most uptight?
Uh, Denver.
Boulder and Denver, they're really uptight, man.
Boulder are real fucking uptight people.
Um, Durango is really uptight.
Stuck up?
Stuck up?
Like, people who think that they're better than everybody?
Boulder.
For sure.
I've met lots of people like that in Boulder.
Have you hooked up with the ladies in your travels in the van?
No.
If I would, though, it would be, like,
A girl who works at Starbucks,
The desk at Starbucks or a gym.
Is there anything...
Oh, wait.
I went dancing at a disco when I was in, um, Sacramento.
And there was this big black girl who I was, like, dancing with a little bit.
And this other one, too.
And that was the only time that I went out.
Is there anything you would wish you would have done differently since you began your road trip?
Um, I brought too much stuff.
When I first started my road trip, I wanted to do it with my car.
I mean, my bike.
I wanted to ride my bike all over the place.
So I brought bike stuff.
And so now I've got all this bike stuff that I don't need.
And, um, I have my big rocket box on the roof.
And I have my bike on the roof.
So when I pass through home again in, like, February or something,
I don't know when that's going to be exactly.
But, um, I'm going to, um, make sure...
I'm going to get rid of this rocket box
And, uh, put the bike on a bike rack on the back of the car
So that I can take it off and put it on really quickly.
And then get in the habit of riding my bike all around town.
So I drive my car less and spend less money that way.
And get some exercise, you know,
In the habit of riding a bike around and exploring stuff.
And, uh, just have everything that I own right here in the car with me
And, um, pack it really well and not have stuff that I don't need.
And, um, yeah.
I wish that when I had money, I would have set up my car a little bit better.
Um, I don't want to get an extra battery, car battery,
And then, uh, like, a switch that connects the main battery to the extra battery
Because then the extra battery is going to get charged when I drive it, drive the car.
And then I can cook up the extra battery there to my inverter
And then actually have electricity, you know.
Because I barely have electricity now.
My little solar panel's not charging.
Maybe my RV battery's dead or something.
But, um, so, yeah.
That's about that.
Um, yeah, but that's the cool thing about traveling around
Is you get to notice how people are different in different places.
Yeah.
So as far as the niceness of people, yeah.
They were kind of, they weren't very nice in Sacramento.
They were, like, grumpy in Sacramento.
Um, and there were, I guess, uh, so-so in Tucson.
Really friendly in Phoenix.
Really friendly in, uh, all over Washington.
Like, off the charts friendly in Las Vegas.
Uh, I guess those are all the places that I've been so far.
But, yeah, good subject.
Okay, that's that.
Quick video.
Quick video.
Quick video.
Quick video.
Quick video.
